description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between the rough, light-colored stone and dark recessed channels. The block's sides are smooth and solid, with the grooves only present on the top face. The stone appears dense and heavy, with a matte, slightly rough finish that suggests durability and resistance to wear. The overall appearance is utilitarian, likely designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible markings, engravings, or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional purpose.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its top surface, alternating between light stone and dark channels. The sides are smooth and the finish is matte and rough.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide face with horizontal grooves and alternating light and dark stripes.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooved pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no signs of erosion or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
